title: Refunds & Changes
heroImageAlt: An open travel notebook and pencil on a vintage world map with a camera
intro: Plans change. Here is how date changes, cancellations and refunds work on FlyAir — including the standard processing windows you can expect.

sections.p:
Eligible fares can be cancelled free of charge within 24 hours of original booking for a full refund — this includes the base fare, taxes and any FlyAir service fees. Look for the "Free 24h cancellation" badge before you pay.

If you cancel within this window, request the cancellation from Manage Booking or by writing to info@flyair.lk with your booking reference. Refunds to card are released the same day; the bank typically settles within 5 to 10 working days.

You can request a date, time or routing change at any time. The cost is made up of two parts: (1) the airline change fee set by the operating carrier and (2) any fare difference between your original fare and the new one. Both numbers are shown before you confirm.

Name changes are generally not permitted on most airlines — please enter passenger names exactly as on the passport at the time of booking.

Eligibility and the refundable amount depend on the fare rules of your ticket. Most discounted economy fares are non-refundable but allow a partial refund of taxes; premium and flexible fares can usually be refunded with smaller penalties.

We never charge a markup on a refund. You receive whatever the airline returns to us, minus any non-refundable airline charges or our service fee disclosed at the time of the request.

sections.h: When the airline cancels or changes your flight
sections.p:
When the operating airline cancels a flight or makes a significant schedule change (typically more than 60 minutes), you are entitled to one of: rebooking on the next available service at no extra cost, rerouting via an alternative airport, or a full refund. The applicable options are set by the airline and any passenger-rights regulations of the country involved.

We will contact you using the details on your booking as soon as we learn of a change, and walk you through the options.

Once the operating airline confirms a refund to us, we initiate the return to your original payment method the same working day. The bank or card issuer then needs:
• Credit card: 5 to 14 working days to show in your statement.
• Debit card: 7 to 21 working days.
• Bank transfer: 1 to 3 working days.

We email you when the refund is initiated and again when the airline settles it on our side.

The fastest route is Manage Booking → "Cancel or change" with your booking reference. You can also email info@flyair.lk with your reference and the change you want; for time-sensitive requests please also call 0788 788 788.

If your trip was insured (through us or directly), some non-refundable losses can be claimed back from the insurer — for example trip cancellation due to illness or family emergency, depending on policy terms. We can issue the airline’s non-refund letter for your insurance claim on request.
